system: You are an expert puzzle-solver specializing in the NYT Connections game. Follow the rules strictly and provide structured responses.
user:
HOW TO PLAY 1. You are given 16 words that form 4 hidden groups of 4 related words each. 2. Sort all 16 words into the 4 groups they belong to. 3. Every word must be used exactly once. Each group must contain EXACTLY 4 words.
BONUS Some puzzles hide a TRAP: a 5th set of 4 words, drawn from the same 16, that forms a convincing category but is not one of the 4 real groups. Playing it as a group would leave the puzzle unsolvable, because its words actually belong to different real groups. If a trap exists, identify its 4 words. A trap never takes 3 of its words from a single real group.
SCORING This is a One-Shot Puzzle. You get to submit your response exactly once with no feedback. Base: 1 point per matched group; a perfect solve (all 4 groups) scores 3. Trap bonus: - +2 if your trap set is a genuine designed trap. - +2 if you state N/A and the puzzle truly has no traps. Maximum: 5 points per puzzle. If your answer is not in a valid format — not exactly 4 groups of 4 words, or the 16 words do not exactly match the puzzle's words — you score 0.
RESPONSE FORMAT: Provide your response in this exact structure:
[Your reasoning]
[Four lines, each with EXACTLY four words, ALL CAPS, comma-separated — one line per group]
[ONE line with EXACTLY four words, ALL CAPS, comma-separated — your single most likely trap set. Or state N/A; not all puzzles have traps.]
[A number between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating your confidence in this answer]
